mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflect expanding labor demand, which boosts consumer income and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of PAYEMS change vs. 20-day forward return remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS changes coincide with negative SPX returns more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) crossing above a high threshold (e.g., 6%) signals severe labor slack, reducing aggregate demand and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE > 6% but SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of instances over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: The differential between the federal funds rate and CPI inflation (real interest rate) determines the discount rate for equities; negative real rates (FEDFUNDS below trailing CPI growth) signal monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations and produces positive forward S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the FEDFUNDS minus CPI-12mo spread vs. 20-day forward return is below 0.05 absolute value over any 3-year window, or the signal is not consistent across expansion and recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment defined by FEDFUNDS below 1%, housing starts (HOUST) rise in response to cheap credit, signaling residential investment strength that feeds into GDP and corporate earnings, positively predicting S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 1%, the rank correlation between HOUST and SPX forward 60-day returns is less than 0.10 over a 5-year sample, or the directional hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in industrial production (INDPRO) indicate expanding aggregate supply, which raises corporate output and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The rank IC of INDPRO monthly change vs. 20-day forward SPX return remains below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO changes coincide with negative SPX returns more than 45% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1000 thousand units (annualized), it signals a housing market contraction that depresses construction activity, consumer wealth, and aggregate demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If HOUST < 1000 but SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) provide a composite signal of robust consumer demand, which directly lifts corporate revenues and earnings, positively predicting S&P 500 returns over a 20-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint increase (both series rising month-over-month) predicts SPX direction with a hit rate below 55% over any 3-year window, or the signal is not additive beyond each series individually (interaction not statistically significant).

mechanism_narrative: An unusually high month-over-month increase in the CPI (CPIAUCSL) signals rising inflation pressure, which may prompt tighter monetary policy, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 5-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If CPI m/m > 0.3% but SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances over a 3-year window, or the average 5-day return is not statistically negative (t-test p > 0.05), the hypothesis is rejected.
